items.title: XRP climbs 8% as record holder losses signal better risk-reward for buyers
items.description: The token's 30-day and 365-day MVRV, a measure of how far holders are underwater, sit near -45% and -47%, lows Santiment says XRP has never reached before. Some traders read stretched losses as a contrarian signal.

items.title: Bitcoin’s next parabolic run may need $1 trillion in fresh capital
items.description: This cycle, about $697 billion in new money has generated a roughly 689% gain, compared with earlier cycles where far less capital drove returns of upto 50,000%.

items.title: This sanctioned Russian stablecoin claims it processes billions, but blockchain analysts disagree
items.description: A7A5 claims crypto data providers understate its trading activity, while blockchain analytics firms say the ruble-backed token's volumes have fallen sharply this year.

items.title: Trump says there is ‘nothing wrong’ with family’s crypto windfall
items.description: The president reported at least $1.4 billion in crypto income for 2025. He told CNBC he did nothing illegal and was not aware of the extent of his holdings. Critics say he is profiting from the office while his administration writes the industry's rules.

items.title: Bitcoin whales bought $16.7 billion of bitcoin in 2 weeks even as ETFs bled a record $4 billion
items.description: U.S. institutional demand had its worst month ever in June. Large holders absorbed the selling, marking a divergence that has shown up near past cycle bottoms.

items.title: Bitcoin, ether traders aren't fully buying the bounce, options markets show
items.description: Your day-ahead look for July 3, 2026

items.title: Crypto bulls on firmer footing as U.S. rate-hike risk recedes
items.description: Crypto ended the week in a more buoyant state after weak U.S. jobs data reduced the chances of a Fed interest-rate increase and Uniswap gained on a link-up with Robinhood.

items.title: Memory and semiconductor stocks lose momentum, bitcoin rebounds in sign of changing investor focus
items.description: After dominating markets in 2026, AI-tied memory and semiconductor stocks are losing momentum, raising the question whether capital will shift back into bitcoin.

items.title: Tim Draper denies moving Bitcoin, reiterates $250,000 BTC prediction
items.description: Tim Draper denies moving Bitcoin after blockchain analysts linked him to a wallet that transferred 1,000 BTC to Coinbase Prime.

items.title: Bitcoin profit and loss ratio falls to 43-month low
items.description: Bitwise chief investment officer Matt Hougan said the bottom is “closer than ever,” while a Swan Bitcoin analyst suggested investors buy now at a discount rather than overpaying later.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: US law enforcement group drops opposition to CLARITY Act: Report
items.description: The Major County Sheriffs of America said it still wants the CLARITY Act amended to give local law enforcement more resources to investigate illicit finance cases.

items.title: Bitcoin, Ether extend relief rallies as extreme fear meets renewed ETF buying
items.description: Bitcoin and Ether bounced off multi-year lows as dip buyers finally stepped in, and the spot BTC ETFs saw a $221 million inflow on July 2.

items.title: SOL rallies as Solana memecoins, prediction market activity surge: Are bulls back?
items.description: Rising interest in Solana-network memecoins and prediction markets was followed by a rally in SOL price. Will the momentum continue?

items.title: US senator calls for ban on elected officials issuing memecoins
items.description: The restriction proposed by Senator Kirsten Gillibrand would bar members of Congress, the US president and their spouses from “issuing or sponsoring their own digital assets.”

items.title: Donald Trump says ‘nothing wrong’ with $1.4B crypto windfall while in office
items.description: The US President disclosed earning more than $1 billion from crypto ventures amid discussions of a digital asset market structure bill in Congress and legislation to ban CBDCs awaiting his desk.

items.title: 5 takeaways from President Donald Trump's interview with CNBC
items.description: The president defended his family's business and said he feels bad that the presidency creates a conflict of interest in any of his children's investments.

items.title: Iran begins six-day funeral for Ayatollah Khamenei nearly four months after his death
items.description: Iran plans a six-day funeral for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, with ceremonies across Iran and Iraq before burial in Mashhad.

items.title: First known congressional SpaceX stock buys surface after record IPO
items.description: The SpaceX stock purchases come as Elon Musk’s company deepens its role in federal contracting and remains closely tied to President Donald Trump’s Washington.

items.title: Ford achieves quality milestone, as CEO targets flawless new vehicle launches
items.description: Ford CEO Jim Farley told CNBC that the automaker has learned from its past quality and recall issues, which have hurt its earnings and stained its reputation.

items.title: Feds seek lower prison term for $100M New Jersey deli fraudster — but some reasons why are hidden
items.description: James Patten is the third person who will be sentenced in the stock manipulation scheme related to Hometown International, a company that owned just one deli.

items.title: Extreme heat wave threatens U.S. power grids and July 4 travel
items.description: The heat wave threatens to overwhelm U.S. grids and may force some to change their plans on one of the busiest travel weeks of the year.

items.title: Trump’s Freedom 250 draws corporate sponsors with business before his administration
items.description: Companies with federal interests — including Boeing, Lockheed Martin and Oracle — are sponsoring a Trump-aligned effort celebrating America’s 250th birthday.

items.title: Oman walks a diplomatic tightrope over Strait of Hormuz fees, creating a ‘blind spot’ for markets
items.description: Oman is using strategic ambiguity as talks over possible Strait of Hormuz fees raise legal, diplomatic and oil market concerns.

Strategy’s bitcoin trading policy creates risks for broader markets, says JPMorgan

My fellow American: who should be remembered for the next 250 years?
items.description: Artists, thinkers and leaders from the US and beyond pick the person they think deserves to be honoured, from Abraham Lincoln to Dorothy Parker

items.title: Stephen Breyer: Can we keep this great experiment?
items.description: There are some who fear not, but I am optimistic. The students I meet are interested in defending their Constitution

items.title: Nicole Krauss: On the strangeness of pledging allegiance to the flag
items.description: An irreconcilable sense of alienation and belonging has been one of America’s many gifts to me

items.title: Simon Schama: The founding fathers and the battle for America’s future
items.description: In 1776, visions of liberty and justice were fraught with compromise, conflict and contradiction. Today, the founders’ words still shape national identity

items.title: Yankee pyramids: how to build a presidential library
items.description: Every modern US president gets a physical totem of his tenure. What can they teach us about America’s struggle to remember itself?

items.title: What has America ever done for us?
items.description: Jazz? The moon landings? Gerrymandering? On this 250th anniversary, it seems only right to think about this question

items.title: Trump uses Mount Rushmore speech to warn of ‘communist menace’ in US
items.description: President kicked off 250th anniversary events with a preview of a likely line of attack against Democrats in coming elections

items.title: An American birthday worth celebrating
items.description: Though its founding ideals are threatened, the US has the capacity for renewal

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1PQsHd1bgwg! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Here’s what’s worth streaming in July 2026 on Netflix, Hulu, HBO Max and more
items.description: Why it’s a good month to save on streaming, despite the return of ‘Enola Holmes’ to Netflix and ‘Silo’ to Apple

items.title: Record beef imports are flooding the U.S. — so why is your Fourth of July BBQ so expensive?
items.description: Beef prices are sizzling and Washington’s recipe for high costs — import more meat — only grills your wallet.

items.title: Your aging parent has a new romance. Here’s how to protect your inheritance — without looking greedy.
items.description: Before your single mom or dad remarries, talk with them about estate-planning issues.

items.title: Opening a ‘Trump account’ for your children? Here is the risk you need to reckon with first.
items.description: The new accounts ban bonds and international stocks, forcing parents to bet their children’s financial future entirely on U.S. equities.

items.title: ‘I don’t think I’ll make it to 80’: I’m 70 and single. Do I take out a reverse mortgage or a home-equity agreement?
items.description: “I’m torn between these two options.”

items.title: Nvidia is betting on a trillion-dollar robotics boom. Here is the hidden way to trade it.
items.description: CEO Jensen Huang has called humanoid robots a “multitrillion-dollar economic opportunity.”

items.title: Want to live longer in retirement? This one money move could add years to your life.
items.description: A secure retirement portfolio acts like a health shield, calming market anxiety that can actually shorten life expectancy.

items.title: Before you max out your 401(k) this year, consider a much better use for your next paycheck
items.description: Grabbing an employer match is essential, but prioritizing high-interest debt and emergency cash can bring much better near-term results.

2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: ‘Heat dome’ over eastern US sends electricity prices soaring
items.description: More than 800,000 households left without power after storms and temperatures near 40C straining utility grids

items.title: Trump uses Mount Rushmore speech to warn of ‘communist menace’ in US
items.description: President kicked off 250th anniversary events with a preview of a likely line of attack against Democrats in coming elections

items.title: America at 250
items.description: What does the US see when it looks in the mirror? As a nation of impossible ambition celebrates its founding, a special edition of FT Weekend Magazine asks writers, thinkers and leaders to reflect on the first 250 years

items.title: UK farmers embrace regenerative methods after heatwave shock
items.description: High temperatures are leading farmers to cut out chemicals and intensive ploughing to improve soil quality so it retains water

items.title: Good vibes are masking a reset in markets
items.description: Stock market returns should not distract investors from the new AI trade taking hold

items.title: It is investors vs gamers as Sony ditches discs
items.description: The move is being presented as zeitgeist rather than greedy rent extraction

items.title: Lack of data puts England’s ‘mansion tax’ valuations at risk
items.description: Research finds around two-fifths of homes valued at £1.5mn-plus have no Land Registry sales records

items.title: Burnham must learn from Starmer’s failures on UK growth
items.description: There are moments to prioritise other things, but after 18 years of historic economic slowdown this is not one of them

items.title: Nearly a Million Investors Lost a Total of $3.8 Billion on Trump Crypto Coin
items.description: A report from a cryptocurrency analytics firm details how those who bought the Trump memecoin have fared, with most retail investors having lost money while sophisticated traders did better.

items.title: Are the ‘MANGOS’ Stocks Already Turning Soft?
items.description: Wall Street loves an acronym. The latest one stands for Meta, Anthropic, Nvidia and three other companies at the center of the artificial intelligence boom.

items.title: The Rise of Racetrack Country Clubs: ‘Racing Is Golf on Steroids’
items.description: A motorsports club north of Miami, on former orange groves, is being built to cater to enthusiasts and well-heeled novices eager to hit the gas and pull some Gs.

items.title: David S. Doty, Judge Who Helped Shape the Modern N.F.L., Dies at 96
items.description: He presided over the 1993 agreement that led to free agency and the salary cap, and for years oversaw the league’s disputes with its players.

items.title: Why Some Banks Still Charge High Overdraft Fees
items.description: Congress scrapped a rule last year that capped how much banks could charge if you spent more than was in your account. Now, banks’ revenue from the fees is rising.

items.title: Will Late-Night TV Work on YouTube?
items.description: Julian Shapiro-Barnum, best known for a social media series called “Recess Therapy,” has started an online-only late-night show.

items.title: Why One Global Shipping Choke Point Isn’t a Blueprint for Another
items.description: The Strait of Malacca may be a model for how Oman and Iran could collect fees in the Strait of Hormuz, but the differences between the waterways are vast.

items.title: The Hottest Stock Markets Lead to the Biggest Losses
items.description: Investor enthusiasm culminated in some of the worst cases of wealth destruction in the last 100 years, a long-running study shows.
